no_budget.instructions: Agree that today is not about budget. Pivot to Title II urgency. Position the demo as knowledge-gathering for when budget opens. Emphasize the cost of a lawsuit is 10-100x the cost of a solution.
example_responses: Totally fair. This wouldn't be a budget conversation — more just getting you up to speed on what's out there before the deadline hits. Even 20 minutes.
Makes sense. But just to put it in perspective — a single DOJ complaint costs more to defend than a year of compliance tooling. Might be worth at least seeing what's out there.
Understood. When does your next budget cycle open? Might make sense to at least have a conversation now so you're ready when it does.

already_compliant.instructions: Don't argue. Acknowledge. Then probe for PDFs specifically — that's almost always the gap. Ask about their document library, not just the web pages.
example_responses: That's great. Are you covering the document side too — PDFs, board minutes, agendas? That's usually where we see the gap.
Good to hear. When was the last time you ran a full scan against WCAG 2.1 AA? The Title II updates added some new requirements.
Nice. Is your compliance covering just the main site, or are you scanning subdomains and third-party apps too?

website_redesign.instructions: Frame this as perfect timing. It's much easier and cheaper to build accessibility in during a redesign than to retrofit later. Ask who is handling the redesign.
example_responses: Perfect timing actually. It's way easier to build accessibility in during the redesign than to bolt it on after. Who's handling the build?
That's actually ideal. A lot of our customers time EqualGround with their redesign so the new site launches fully compliant from day one. Who's the vendor?
Smart. So the question is — is the new site being built to Title II standards, or are you planning to address that separately?

using_competitor.instructions: Don't bash the competitor directly. Ask how it's working for them. Probe for gaps — especially PDFs, automation, and whether the tool flags vs. fixes. Use the relevant battlecard from battlecards.json.
example_responses: Got it. How's it been working for you? Is it catching everything, or are there areas where you're still doing manual work?
OK. And does it handle your PDFs and documents too, or mainly just the web pages?
When you say you're using it — is it scanning and flagging, or is it actually remediating the issues for you?

send_email.instructions: Always agree to send an email. But before hanging up, anchor a follow-up: offer to include the scan results and ask for a specific follow-up time.
example_responses: Happy to. What's the best email? And would it help if I included the scan results from your site so you can see what we flagged?
Sure thing. I'll send that over. Would it make sense to block 15 minutes later this week to walk through what I send? That way I can answer questions live.
Absolutely. I'll put together the scan results and a quick overview. What's a good email, and is there a day this week I could follow up?

trigger_phrases: need to talk to my boss
I'd need to run it by
not_my_decision.instructions: Ask who else is involved. Offer to include them on the demo. Position yourself as helping the prospect build the internal case.
example_responses: Totally get it. Who else would need to be in the conversation for this to move forward?
Makes sense. Would it help if I set up the Zoom so they could join too? Sometimes it's easier to make the case with a live walkthrough.
Understood. Would it help if I put together a one-pager you could share with them? Or would it be better to just loop them into a quick call?

too_busy.instructions: Respect their time immediately. Offer a very specific alternative time. Keep it to 15/ 20 minutes. Make it easy to say yes.
example_responses: Totally get it. I'll keep this quick — would 15 minutes later this week be easier? I just want to share what the scan found on your site.
No worries at all. When would be a better time? I'm flexible — even a 10-minute call works.
Understood. Could I call you back Thursday afternoon? It'll be quick — just want to share the scan results.
